response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with deep web and full-stack experience, but he misses the role's hard requirements: no Japanese proficiency is listed (native-level is mandatory), his C work is brief and dates to 2007-2008 with no UNIX/C focus since, and there is no evidence of Linux kernel analysis or modification. He also shows no background in L2/L3 networking, switch products, or the security technologies central to the role (MACsec, 802.1X, authentication systems). Embedded exposure is limited to passing mentions. Strong engineer, wrong profile for this position.
strengths: 20+ years of software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Strong engineering craft: TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks, and low-latency systems work
Comfortable in Linux environments and polyglot across many languages
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed; native-level Japanese is a hard requirement
C for UNIX experience is minimal and outdated; no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience
No Layer-2/Layer-3 networking, network-switch, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) background

key_factors.factor: No C/UNIX, Linux kernel, or embedded-networking depth
Broad senior engineering experience and testing discipline
